author: doll-agent
content:
Done. All five Poly impls (Polynomial in power.rs, Chebyshev, Legendre, Hermite, HermiteE, Laguerre) used a naive O(n) loop. mul is the expensive primitive (O(n^2) for power basis; routed through power-basis conversion for orthogonal bases). Replaced with binary exponentiation that squares the running base and accumulates only when the current exponent bit is 1. For n = 100, multiply count drops from 99 to ~13. Same n==1 fast-path short-circuit added to all six files.

Added 2 cross-check tests in power.rs:
- pow_one_returns_self: validates the n==1 short-circuit.
- pow_high_exponent_matches_naive_loop: n=11 (binary 0b1011, both odd and even bits) checked against the analytical binomial expansion of (1+x)^11.

ferray-polynomial: 133 -> 135 tests, all passing, clippy clean. Commit ed4cd2a0.
